padding_oracle_cbc.description: WSTG-CRYP-02 -- CBC-mode ciphertext (cookie, view-state, encrypted-token) reveals padding validity via differential response (200 vs 500, or distinct error text). Vaudenay attack: byte-level plaintext recovery + arbitrary ciphertext forging.

probes.description: Flip the last byte of a base64/hex ciphertext-looking value -- distinct response between 'padding invalid' (500) and 'mac/decrypt OK but business-logic fail' (400/200) reveals oracle. Confirm with 3 flipped variants.
Detect ASP.NET view-state padding oracle (CVE-2010-3332 lineage) -- ViewState parameter present + behaviour delta on flipped last byte

weak_hash_in_token.description: WSTG-CRYP-04 -- MD5/SHA-1 fingerprint visible in session token, CSRF token, reset-link signature. MD5 length=32 hex, SHA-1 length=40 hex -- collidable/preimage-attackable depending on use

probes.description: Response or cookie contains 32-hex-char value used as auth/CSRF token -- likely MD5 (collision-attackable for forgery)
Response or cookie contains 40-hex-char value used as auth/CSRF token -- likely SHA-1 (chosen-prefix collision practical since 2020)

weak_jwt_alg_hs256_with_predictable_secr.description: WSTG-CRYP-04 / WSTG-SESS-10 -- JWT signed with HS256 and short/predictable secret. Brute-forceable offline if secret is dictionary word, env-default, or service name

probes.description: JWT detected with HS256 -- pull token, attempt hashcat -m 16500 with rockyou + service-name-based wordlist offline. Tool layer flags presence; cracking is offline.

des_3des_rc4_in_response.description: WSTG-CRYP-04 -- response advertises DES / 3DES / RC4 / EXPORT cipher (Server header, Set-Cookie, custom debug). Often combined with weak TLS (WSTG-CRYP-01) audited offline via nmap.

probes.description: Response mentions DES/3DES/RC4/EXPORT/NULL cipher in debug header or body

encrypted_blob_without_integrity.description: Token looks encrypted (base64 long + high entropy) but client can modify and submit -- no HMAC over ciphertext. Reveals via padding-oracle or bit-flip giving 200 instead of 500.

probes.description: Flip a byte mid-ciphertext -- if server returns 200 (or distinct non-padding error) the blob is unauthenticated (no HMAC wrapper)
